user_message: Can you work with me on consumption trend for Nod Publishers?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-26 11:13 EST
Subject: consumption trend - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 17:52 EST
Subject: URGENT: consumption trend Issue at Nod Publishers

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-G trap (Acknowledgment draft over-commits MS deliverables); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-G failure mode (Acknowledgment draft over-commits MS deliverables)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-G -- Acknowledgment draft over-commits MS deliverables
